message:
feat(cli): improve invalid checkpoint handling, offline session resolution, and spawn prompt dispatch

CLI checkpoint commands (list, delete, verify, show, export):
- Surface invalid checkpoint IDs alongside valid entries in both human and
  JSON output, giving operators visibility into corrupt on-disk state
  without requiring manual filesystem inspection
- Delete command now handles invalid checkpoint entries: when Load fails
  but the path exists on disk, it offers to delete the corrupt entry
  rather than erroring with "checkpoint not found"
- Verify command now returns non-zero exit code when verification fails in
  JSON mode, fixing a gap where --json silently returned success for
  invalid checkpoints
- Show command distinguishes "not found" from "exists but corrupt" for
  exact checkpoint IDs, providing actionable error messages

CLI spawn/rollback/root:
- Extract prompt resolution out of per-pane goroutines into the main thread
  via resolveSpawnPanePrompt(), fixing a race condition where marching
  orders and default prompts were resolved concurrently without
  synchronization
- Stagger scheduling now checks hasPrompt (resolved) instead of
  opts.Prompt (global), correctly scheduling staggered delivery for panes
  with marching orders or default prompts even when no global --prompt
  is set
- Add resolveRobotOfflineCapableSession() for ensemble commands that should
  work with persisted state when the tmux session is no longer live
- Rollback command now distinguishes "not found" from "corrupt" for
  checkpoint references matching valid ID format
- Monitor launch deduplication simplified: skip re-launch if existing
  monitor is alive instead of always kill+relaunch
- Marching orders documentation updated to clarify "agent pane order"
  (0-based, excluding user pane) vs raw pane index

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
